And if it is, how should we go about teaching them to do so? As early as 1950, Alan Turing stated that we ought to teach machines in the way of teaching a child.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["[2303.02891] Perspectives on the Social Impacts of Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback Skip to main content We gratefully acknowledge support from the Simons Foundation, member institutions , and all contributors.", "As early as 1950, Alan Turing stated that we ought to teach machines in the way of teaching a child."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: [2303.02891] Perspectives on the Social Impacts of Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback Skip to main content We gr"}, {"url": "https://stars.library.ucf.edu/hut2024/212/", "title": "Reinforcement Learning From Human Feedback For Ethically Robust Ai Decision-Making", "summary": "Keywords Ethical AI Decision-Making; Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF); AI Ethics and Human Values; Robust; Deep Reinforcement Learning; Artificial Intelligence Abstract The emergence of reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) has made great strides toward giving AI decision-making the ability to learn from external human advice. In general, this machine learning technique is concerned with producing agents that learn to work toward optimizing and achieving some goal, advanced by interactions with the environment and feedback given in terms of a quantifiable reward. In the scope of this project, we seek to merge the intricate realms of AI robustness, ethical decision-making, and RLHF.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Keywords Ethical AI Decision-Making; Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF); AI Ethics and Human Values; Robust; Deep Reinforcement Learning; Artificial Intelligence Abstract The emergence of reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) has made great strides toward giving AI decision-making the ability to learn from external human advice.", "In general, this machine learning technique is concerned with producing agents that learn to work toward optimizing and achieving some goal, advanced by interactions with the environment and feedback given in terms of a quantifiable reward."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Keywords Ethical AI Decision-Making; Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF); AI Ethics and Human Values; Robu"}, {"url": "https://arroma.uiowa.edu/docs/publication/paper_pdf/2025/xiao_et_al_2025.pdf", "title": "Implementation of Human-AI Interaction in Reinforcement Learning: Literature Review and Case Studies", "summary": "Email: shaoping-xiao@uiowa.edu Received: 20 August 2025; Accepted: 25 October 2025 ABSTRACT: Te integration of human factors into artiﬁcial intelligence (AI) systems has emerged as a critical research frontier, particularly in reinforcement learning (RL), where human-AI interaction (HAII) presents both opportunities and challenges. As RL continues to demonstrate remarkable success in model-free and partially observable environ- ments, its real-world deployment increasingly requires eﬀective collaboration with human operators and stakeholders. Tis article systematically examines HAII techniques in RL through both theoretical analysis and practical case studies.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Email: shaoping-xiao@uiowa.edu Received: 20 August 2025; Accepted: 25 October 2025 ABSTRACT: Te integration of human factors into artiﬁcial intelligence (AI) systems has emerged as a critical research frontier, particularly in reinforcement learning ", "A key research goal within this context is the development of AI-driven autonomous systems Copyright © 2025 Te Authors.", "Tis work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License, which permits unrestricted use, distribution, and reproduction in any medium, provided the original work is properly cited. 1VCMJTIFE0OMJOF/PWFNCFS 2 Comp", "Tese systems hold the potential to revolutionize industries by enabling faster responses, reducing human errors, and maintaining continuous operation in dynamic or hazardous environments [6].", "As environments evolve or increase in complexity, RL agents continuously reﬁne their policies to improve performance."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Email: shaoping-xiao@uiowa.edu Received: 20 August 2025; Accepted: 25 October 2025 ABSTRACT: Te integration of human fac"}, {"url": "https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-025-92889-7", "title": "A framework for mitigating malicious RLHF feedback in LLM training using consensus based reward", "summary": "Abstract Large Language models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities in natural language processing and understanding. LLMs are being rapidly adopted in major industry sectors including mobile computing, healthcare, finance, government, and education driven by technology giants such as NVIDIA, OpenAI, Microsoft, Apple, Meta, Google, Broadcom, AMD, and IBM. However, due to the emerging nature of this technology, many security/privacy challenges remain unresolved that we must tackle before rolling out LLMs to critical applications (e.g.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["However, RLHF can also expose the LLM to malicious feedback generated by one or more individuals in the process leading to degraded performance of the LLM and harmful responses.", "Apple Intelligence (utilizing OpenAI’s ChatGPT) is being integrated into all MacBooks with M1 chips (or later) and all new iPhones.", "Microsoft Copilot is being shipped with most new Windows computers and Meta’s AI assistant is using Llama 3.2 under the hood.", "Integrating LLMs in these critical applications may or may not have certain benefits but we must consider the security/privacy implications before such integrations are rolled out.", "However, a malicious individual (or group) involved in the RLHF process can significantly compromise the reward model which in turn can hamper the LLM performance and trustworthiness."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: However, RLHF can also expose the LLM to malicious feedback generated by one or more individuals in the process leading "}, {"url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.15217", "title": "Open problems and fundamental limitations of reinforcement learning from human feedback", "summary": "Computer Science > Artificial Intelligence [Submitted on 27 Jul 2023 (v1), last revised 11 Sep 2023 (this version, v2)] Title:Open Problems and Fundamental Limitations of Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback View PDFAbstract:Reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) is a technique for training AI systems to align with human goals. RLHF has emerged as the central method used to finetune state-of-the-art large language models (LLMs). Despite this popularity, there has been relatively little public work systematizing its flaws.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Computer Science > Artificial Intelligence [Submitted on 27 Jul 2023 (v1), last revised 11 Sep 2023 (this version, v2)] Title:Open Problems and Fundamental Limitations of Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback View PDFAbstract:Reinforcement learn", "Submission history From: Stephen Casper [view email][v1] Thu, 27 Jul 2023 22:29:25 UTC (921 KB) [v2] Mon, 11 Sep 2023 17:25:24 UTC (939 KB) Current browse context: cs.AI References & Citations export BibTeX citation Loading...", "A 300-400 word deep analysis focused on findings relevant to the research question, including:
   - Key data points, statistics, and numerical findings
   - Methodology or approach described (if any)
   - Contradictions or tensions with commonly know", "Which STORM perspectives are covered (from: Scientific, Regulatory, Industry, Economic, Public_Health, Historical, Regional, Methodological, Emerging_Trends)
3. 3-5 specific facts with numbers, dates, or named entities
4.", "Any data tables, statistics, or quantitative results (preserve exact numbers)
5."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Computer Science > Artificial Intelligence [Submitted on 27 Jul 2023 (v1), last revised 11 Sep 2023 (this version, v2)] "}]}
